title: adenanthos pungens subsp. effusus
intro: Adenanthos pungens subsp. effusus, also known as the coastal bush or smoke bush, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Proteaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Adenanthos pungens subsp. effusus include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and soothing respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol to release its medicinal compounds.
